volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Persians (Section 2.967 - Section 7.1059)

sections.label: Section 2.967
sections.translation: Woe ! woe ! cry aloud ! Where is your beloved Pharnuchus , and the courageous Ariomardus ? Where is prince Seualces ,

sections.label: Section 2.970
sections.translation: or Lilaeus of noble lineage , Memphis , Tharybis and Masistras , Artembares and Hystaechmas ? This I ask you .

sections.label: Section 3.974
sections.translation: Woe , woe is me !

sections.label: Section 3.975
sections.translation: They beheld ancient and hateful Athens and with one convulsive struggle ( alas , alas !) poor wretches , they lie gasping on the shore .

sections.label: Section 3.978
sections.translation: Did you really lose your trusty eye [*] there , that which

sections.label: Section 3.980
sections.translation: counted tens upon tens of thousands of the Persians , Batanochus ’ son Alpistus . . . son of Sesames , Megabates ’ son , Parthos and mighty Oebares , did you leave these behind ?

sections.label: Section 3.985
sections.translation: Alas , alas , the unhappy men ! You speak of woe , surpassing woe , for noble Persians .

sections.label: Section 3.988
sections.translation: In truth you stir yearning in me for my gallant comrades

sections.label: Section 3.990
sections.translation: when you tell me of woe , surpassing woe , hateful and unforgettable . The heart within me cries out aloud for the wretched ones .

sections.label: Section 3.992
sections.translation: And yet there are others too whom we miss : Xanthis , commander of a myriad Mardian men , and warlike Anchares ,

sections.label: Section 3.995
sections.translation: Diaexis , too , and Arsaces , captains of the horse , Cegdadatas and Lythimnas , and Tolmus , insatiate in war . I marvel ,

sections.label: Section 3.1000
sections.translation: that they do not follow behind around your wheeled tent .

sections.label: Section 4.1002
sections.translation: Gone are they who were leaders of the host .

sections.label: Section 4.1003
sections.translation: Gone , alas , ingloriously .

sections.label: Section 4.1004
sections.translation: Alas , alas !

sections.label: Section 4.1005
sections.translation: Alas , alas , you powers divine , you brought ruin upon us , unexpected , unmistakable to see , as is the glance of calamity .

sections.label: Section 4.1008
sections.translation: We have been stricken by misfortune such as will endure for ages .

sections.label: Section 4.1009
sections.translation: We have been stricken ; it is abundantly clear .

sections.label: Section 4.1010
sections.translation: By strange woe , strange woe !

sections.label: Section 4.1011
sections.translation: It was with bad luck that we encountered Ionia ’ s mariners . Unfortunate in war , indeed , is Persia ’ s race .

sections.label: Section 5.1014
sections.original: πῶς δ οὔ ; στρατὸν μὲν τοσοῦ-
sections.translation: How true it is . In the loss of so great an army

sections.label: Section 5.1015
sections.translation: I have indeed been dealt a blow , wretched as I am .

sections.label: Section 5.1016
sections.original: τί δ οὒκ ὄλωλεν , μέγ ἄλαστε , [*] Περσᾶν ;
sections.translation: What that belonged to Persia , unfortunate one , has not been destroyed ?

sections.label: Section 5.1017
sections.original: ὁρᾷς τὸ λοιπὸν τόδε τᾶς ἐμᾶς στολᾶς ;
sections.translation: Do you see this remnant of my royal robe ?

sections.label: Section 5.1018
sections.translation: Yes , I do indeed .

sections.label: Section 5.1020
sections.translation: And this quiver —

sections.label: Section 5.1021
sections.translation: What is this you say has been saved ?

sections.label: Section 5.1022
sections.translation: Treasury for shafts ?

sections.label: Section 5.1023
sections.translation: Truly a small remnant from an ample store .

sections.label: Section 5.1024
sections.translation: We have been deprived of defenders .

sections.label: Section 5.1025
sections.translation: Ionia ’ s people shrink not from the spear .

sections.label: Section 5.1026
sections.translation: They are a war-like people . I have lived to behold a calamity I never expected .

sections.label: Section 5.1028
sections.translation: Are you speaking of the routed multitude which manned your ships ?

sections.label: Section 5.1030
sections.original: πέπλον δ ἐπέρρηξ ἐπὶ συμφορᾷ κακοῦ .
sections.translation: And I rent my robe at the disastrous event .

sections.label: Section 5.1031
sections.translation: Alas , alas !

sections.label: Section 5.1032
sections.original: καὶ πλέον ἢ παπαῖ μὲν οὖν .
sections.translation: No , far worse than alas .

sections.label: Section 5.1033
sections.translation: Yes , for twofold and threefold —

sections.label: Section 5.1034
sections.translation: Pain to us , but exultation to our enemies

sections.label: Section 5.1035
sections.translation: Yes , and our strength has been maimed .

sections.label: Section 5.1036
sections.translation: I am stripped of my escort .

sections.label: Section 5.1037
sections.translation: By the disasters of your comrades upon the sea .

sections.label: Section 6.1038
sections.original: δίαινε δίαινε πῆμα : πρὸς δόμους δ ἴθι .
sections.translation: Weep , weep over our calamity , and depart to your homes .

sections.label: Section 6.1039
sections.translation: Alas , alas , woe , woe !

sections.label: Section 6.1040
sections.translation: Cry aloud now in response to me .

sections.label: Section 6.1041
sections.translation: A wretched offering from the wretched to the wretched .

sections.label: Section 6.1042
sections.translation: Cry out , tuning your strain to mine .

sections.label: Section 6.1043
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! That which has happened is grievous indeed .

sections.label: Section 6.1045
sections.translation: Alas , for I too share in your sorrow .

sections.label: Section 6.1046
sections.original: ἔρεσσ ἔρεσσε καὶ στέναζ ἐμὴν χάριν .
sections.translation: Strike , strike , and groan for my sake .

sections.label: Section 6.1047
sections.translation: I weep in lamentation !

sections.label: Section 6.1048
sections.translation: Now cry aloud in response to me .

sections.label: Section 6.1049
sections.translation: This care , my lord , is mine .

sections.label: Section 6.1050
sections.translation: Lift up your voice now in lamentation .

sections.label: Section 6.1051
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! And with our wailing , alas , will be mingled bruising blows and shrieks of pain .

sections.label: Section 7.1054
sections.original: καὶ στέρν ἄρασσε καὶ βόα [*] τὸ Μύσιον .
sections.translation: Beat your breast too , and raise the Mysian wail .

sections.label: Section 7.1055
sections.translation: Anguish , anguish !

sections.label: Section 7.1056
sections.original: καί μοι γενείου πέρθε [*] λευκήρη τρίχα .
sections.translation: And tear , I beg of you , the white hair from your beard .

sections.label: Section 7.1057
sections.translation: With clenched nails , with clenched nails , with loud wailing .

sections.label: Section 7.1058
sections.translation: And utter shrill cries .

sections.label: Section 7.1059
sections.translation: This too I will do .
